Support Afghan diaspora organizations working on sustainable agriculture knowledge transfer
i: Note: Under Taliban governance (since August 2021), formal institutional channels are severely constrained. International NGOs operating in Afghanistan include WFP (World Food Programme), FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization), and ICRC (International Committee of the Red Cross). Local organizations like the Afghanistan Research and Evaluation Unit (AREU) continue limited research operations. UNAMA (United Nations Assistance Mission in Afghanistan) monitors human rights and governance.

Afghanistan's formal corporate sector is largely non-functional under current conditions. International engagement occurs through multilateral development organizations and humanitarian agencies. The Afghanistan Investment Authority was established to attract foreign investment but is currently non-operational.

FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization) — maintains agricultural programs in Afghanistan focused on food security and sustainable farming. UNODC (United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime) — publishes annual Afghanistan Opium Survey tracking cultivation area, yield, and farm-gate prices. World Bank Afghanistan Reconstruction Trust Fund (ARTF) — the largest single source of development funding for Afghanistan, currently operating in limited capacity under trừng phạt constraints.

Note: Afghanistan under Taliban rule has no elected legislature or representative democratic institutions. International engagement channels include: UN Security Council trừng phạt regime (Resolution 2255), which allows humanitarian exemptions. The Doha process for political dialogue. Afghan diaspora organizations in Europe, North America, and Australia. International development partners coordinate through the Geneva Conference on Afghanistan.

AL.s: ứng cử viên EU country with small open nền kinh tế ($27B GDP). Oil production (1.04% rents) centered in Patos-Marinzë field. Services-dominant (48.7% GDP) with significant hydropower potential (95%+ of electricity from hydro).

sectors.w: Albania's oil sector is small and concentrated in aging onshore fields. As EU accession negotiations require môi trường acquis alignment, the lợi ích chồng chéo between oil producers and government revenue suy yếu — EU môi trường standards make marginal fields unprofitable.
bp: EU accession môi trường alignment eliminates subsidies for marginal oil production. Khi Albania must meet EU môi trường standards, lợi ích chồng chéo trong continued extraction from aging fields (many dating to communist era) disappears — cleanup costs exceed production revenue.

Thực hiện EU-aligned môi trường monitoring and phát thải disclosure. Albania's EITI (Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) membership provides a framework — extending to phát thải makes W visible.
Không thể phá vỡ by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report môi trường contamination at oil production sites to AKM (National Environment Agency)
Document health impacts on communities near oil fields — report to Institute of Public Health
Use Albania's người tố giác protection law (Law 60/2016) for reporting môi trường violations
Albpetrol: Phát triển decommissioning and site remediation plan for aging oil fields
Đầu tư vào năng lượng tái tạo diversification — Albania has significant untapped solar and wind potential
Áp dụng EITI enhanced reporting standards including môi trường metrics
AKM: Thực thi môi trường remediation requirements at legacy oil production sites
AKBN: Require môi trường bonds for all new exploration licenses
ERE (Energy Regulatory Entity): Accelerate năng lượng tái tạo permitting
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn member of the Kuvendi (Albanian Parliament) để yêu cầu môi trường remediation of legacy oil sites
Support accelerated EU accession negotiations contingent on môi trường acquis compliance
Advocate for inclusion of môi trường cleanup costs in Albania's EITI reporting
Thúc đẩy Energy Community Treaty enforcement on năng lượng tái tạo targets
i: AKM (Agjencia Kombëtare e Mjedisit / National Environment Agency) — responsible for môi trường monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. AKM conducts môi trường impact assessments for energy projects. AKBN (Agjencia Kombëtare e Burimeve Natyrore / National Agency of Natural Resources) — regulates oil and gas exploration and production licenses. Law 60/2016 'On Whistleblowing and Whistleblower Protection' — Albania's người tố giác protection statute, adopted as part of EU accession reforms, providing protection against retaliation for reporting violations of law.

Albpetrol — Albania's nhà nước oil company, managing legacy fields from the communist era. Albpetrol's operations include some of Europe's most contaminated industrial sites. Bankers Petroleum (now Geo-Jade Petroleum, Chinese-owned) — the largest oil producer in Albania, operating the Patos-Marinzë field. EITI (Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) — Albania has been an EITI member since 2013, publishing annual reports on oil and khai thác mỏ revenues.

ERE (Enti Rregullator i Energjisë / Energy Regulatory Entity) — Albania's independent cơ quan quản lý năng lượng, responsible for licensing, tariff-setting, and market regulation in the electricity and natural gas sectors. Ministry of Infrastructure and Energy — oversees national energy policy including the National Energy Strategy 2030, which targets significant năng lượng tái tạo expansion.

Kuvendi i Shqipërisë (Parliament of Albania) — unicameral legislature with 140 members elected by proportional representation. Citizens can contact their deputies through parlament.al. The Komisioni për Veprimtarinë Prodhuese (Committee on Production Activities) handles energy and môi trường legislation. Energy Community Treaty — Albania is a member of the Energy Community, which extends EU energy and môi trường acquis to Southeast European countries. Non-compliance can result in dispute settlement proceedings.

AM.s: Small landlocked nền kinh tế ($26B GDP) with significant khai thác mỏ sector (min=6.79% of GDP, primarily copper-molybdenum at Kajaran and Sotk gold mine). Services-dominant (62% GDP). Geopolitical constraints from Nagorno-Karabakh conflict and closed borders with Turkey and Azerbaijan.

sectors.w: Armenia's khai thác mỏ sector generates significant water pollution (Debed and Voghji rivers) and tailings dam risks. Khi downstream water quality data is published alongside mine production revenue, W trở nên có thể tính được from bilateral transactions.
bp: Mandate môi trường bonds and rehabilitation deposits that fully internalize mine closure costs. Khi khai thác mỏ companies must pre-fund môi trường remediation, lợi ích chồng chéo trong extraction without cleanup suy yếu — marginal mines become unprofitable.

Mandatory real-time water quality monitoring downstream of all khai thác mỏ operations, published openly. Armenia's EITI membership provides a disclosure framework — extending to môi trường monitoring data makes W visible from production data.

Có thể phá vỡ một phần. Open-pit khai thác mỏ inherently disturbs landscapes and generates acid mine drainage. However, modern tailings management and water treatment can reduce the magnitude of sự phụ thuộc hệ thống.
a: Report môi trường violations at mine sites to the Environmental and Mining Inspection Body
Document water pollution downstream of tailings facilities — report to the Ministry of Environment
Use Armenia's người tố giác protection provisions under the Anti-Corruption Strategy for reporting
Thực hiện international best practice tailings management (Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management)
Áp dụng EITI enhanced reporting including môi trường impact metrics
Commission independent water quality assessments for all operations
Ministry of Environment: Tăng cường môi trường impact assessment requirements for new khai thác mỏ licenses
EMIB: Increase inspection frequency and enforce penalties for water pollution violations
Ministry of Territorial Administration: Require community consent processes for new mine approvals
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn member of the Azgayin Zhoghov (National Assembly) để yêu cầu mandatory môi trường bonds for all khai thác mỏ operations
Support strengthening of EITI reporting requirements to include môi trường impact data
Advocate for EU-Armenia CEPA (Comprehensive and Enhanced Partnership Agreement) enforcement on môi trường standards
Thúc đẩy international arbitration standards that include môi trường remediation in khai thác mỏ investment disputes
i: Environmental and Mining Inspection Body (EMIB) — the Armenian government agency responsible for monitoring compliance with môi trường regulations at khai thác mỏ operations, conducting inspections, and imposing trừng phạt. Ministry of Environment — oversees môi trường policy, issues môi trường impact assessment approvals, and sets water quality standards. Armenia's Anti-Corruption Council — established under the Prime Minister's office, provides channels for reporting corruption and misconduct in the khai thác mỏ sector.

Zangezur Copper-Molybdenum Combine (ZCMC) — Armenia's largest khai thác mỏ company, operating the Kajaran copper-molybdenum mine. Lydian International / Lydian Armenia — operator of the controversial Amulsar gold mine project. GeoProMining — Russian-owned company operating gold mines in Armenia. EITI — Armenia became an EITI candidate country in 2017, requiring disclosure of khai thác mỏ revenues and contracts.

Ministry of Territorial Administration and Infrastructure — oversees khai thác mỏ policy and licensing. Competition Protection Commission — monitors market concentration in the khai thác mỏ sector. Armenian EITI Multi-Stakeholder Group — includes government, industry, and xã hội dân sự representatives overseeing extractive sector minh bạch.

Azgayin Zhoghov (National Assembly of Armenia) — unicameral parliament with 107 members. Citizens can contact their representatives through parliament.am. Armenia's 2018 revolution established stronger democratic institutions. EU-Armenia CEPA (Comprehensive and Enhanced Partnership Agreement) — signed in 2017, includes provisions on môi trường protection and sustainable development that Armenia has committed to implement.

AO.s: Major African oil producer with 29.28% combined oil and gas rents ($29.6B). Sonangol (state oil company) dominates the nền kinh tế. Agriculture (22.1% GDP) is largely subsistence. Post-civil war recovery continues with high inequality.

sectors.w: Angola's oil production is declining from mature deepwater fields. As extraction costs rise and global demand plateaus, the lợi ích chồng chéo between Sonangol and international partners (TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil) suy yếu — declining reserves make diversification economically rational.
bp: Redirect Sonangol investment from deepwater exploration to năng lượng tái tạo and agricultural modernization. Angola's solar potential is enormous (2,500+ kWh/m²/year). Khi renewable returns exceed marginal barrel returns from declining fields, the lợi ích chồng chéo breaks.

Thực hiện EITI-standard revenue minh bạch extended to per-block môi trường impact data. Angola rejoined EITI in 2020 — extending disclosure to phát thải and môi trường costs makes W computable per production unit.
Không thể phá vỡ by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report môi trường violations at oil facilities to MIREMPET (Ministry of Mineral Resources, Petroleum and Gas)
Document community health impacts near production facilities through local NGOs
Report financial irregularities in Sonangol operations — Angola's anti-corruption framework (Lei 3/20) provides limited protections
Sonangol: Phát triển strategic diversification plan with binding năng lượng tái tạo investment targets
International partners (TotalEnergies, BP): Thực hiện Paris-aligned phát thải reduction plans for Angolan operations
Áp dụng TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all production sharing agreements
ANPG: Require môi trường impact assessments with community consultation for all new production blocks
Ministry of Environment: Tăng cường enforcement of môi trường regulations in the petroleum sector
Banco Nacional de Angola: Integrate climate risk into financial supervision of oil-dependent lending
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn member of the Assembleia Nacional để yêu cầu publication of per-block môi trường impact data for all oil operations
Support EITI enhanced reporting with môi trường metrics for Angola
Vận động qua the African Union for continental năng lượng tái tạo investment targets
Thúc đẩy Paris Agreement compliance monitoring through UNFCCC mechanisms
i: MIREMPET (Ministério dos Recursos Minerais, Petróleo e Gás) — Angola's ministry overseeing oil and gas operations, licensing, and regulation. ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — the national petroleum regulator, created in 2019 to replace Sonangol's dual role as regulator and operator. Lei 3/20 (Law on Public Probity) — Angola's anti-corruption law establishing asset declaration requirements and conflicts of interest rules for public officials. Whistleblower protection remains weak.

Sonangol E.P. — Angola's state oil company and the country's largest enterprise. Sonangol holds equity in all oil production sharing agreements and manages the state's petroleum interests. The company underwent restructuring in 2019, separating its regulatory functions to ANPG. TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil, Eni — major international oil companies operating deepwater blocks offshore Angola.

ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — established in 2019 as the independent petroleum regulator, responsible for managing exploration and production contracts, monitoring compliance with license terms, and administering bidding rounds. Ministério do Ambiente — Angola's environment ministry, responsible for môi trường policy, impact assessments, and pollution control.

Assembleia Nacional (National Assembly of Angola) — unicameral legislature with 220 members. Angola's electoral system uses party-list proportional representation. The most recent elections were held in 2022. Citizens can engage through assembleia.ao. Angola is a presidential republic with significant executive power. EITI — Angola was suspended from EITI in 2017 for insufficient progress but successfully reapplied and was readmitted in 2020, committing to enhanced minh bạch standards.

AR.s: G20 nền kinh tế ($638.4B GDP) with massive Vaca Muerta shale formation (second-largest shale gas, fourth-largest shale oil reserves globally). Agricultural powerhouse (soy, beef) with 5.8% ag GDP share but outsized môi trường footprint. Chronic macroeconomic instability.

sectors.w: Argentina's Vaca Muerta development requires massive capital investment ($10B+/year) and government subsidies (Plan Gas). Removing subsidies breaks the lợi ích chồng chéo — without price guarantees, many Vaca Muerta wells are marginal at global market prices.
bp: Phase out Plan Gas subsidies and redirect capital to năng lượng tái tạo. Argentina has world-class wind resources in Patagonia and solar in the northwest. Khi renewable electricity undercuts subsidized gas, the lợi ích chồng chéo between producers and government in continued fossil expansion breaks.

Mandatory per-well phát thải disclosure including methane venting and flaring. Argentina's Secretaría de Energía already collects production data — extending to phát thải makes W computable from production statistics.
Không thể phá vỡ by policy alone. Shale gas extraction and combustion release CO₂ and methane by physical process.
a: Report môi trường violations at Vaca Muerta operations to the provincial cơ quan môi trường (Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén)
Document water contamination from fracking operations — report to AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas)
Report safety violations to the Secretaría de Trabajo
YPF: Phát triển Paris-aligned transition plan with binding phát thải reduction targets for Vaca Muerta operations
Thực hiện TCFD-aligned climate disclosure across all operations
Đầu tư vào CCUS (carbon capture, utilization, and storage) pilot projects for gas processing facilities
Secretaría de Energía: Publish per-well phát thải data for all Vaca Muerta operations
SAyDS: Tăng cường federal môi trường standards for unconventional hydrocarbon extraction
ENARGAS: Cải cách natural gas tariff structure to eliminate implicit fossil fuel subsidies
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn Diputado Nacional or Senador để yêu cầu a timeline for fossil fuel subsidy phase-out and transparent per-well phát thải reporting
Tăng cường Argentina's NDC — current targets are considered insufficient by Climate Action Tracker
Leverage Mercosur-EU trade agreement môi trường provisions to align energy transition commitments
Support South American năng lượng tái tạo integration through regional grid interconnection
i: Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén — Neuquén province's cơ quan môi trường, responsible for môi trường permitting and monitoring of oil and gas operations in the Vaca Muerta formation. AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas de los ríos Limay, Neuquén y Negro) — the inter-jurisdictional water authority managing the river basins affected by Vaca Muerta operations. COFEMA (Consejo Federal de Medio Ambiente) — federal-provincial môi trường coordination body. Argentina lacks comprehensive federal người tố giác protection legislation, though provincial laws vary.

YPF S.A. — Argentina's largest energy company (51% nhà nước), the dominant operator in Vaca Muerta. YPF's strategic decisions on Vaca Muerta development drive national energy policy. Pan American Energy, Vista Energy, TotalEnergies — other major operators in Vaca Muerta. Plan Gas.Ar — the Argentine government's gas production incentive program, guaranteeing minimum prices for domestic gas production to stimulate Vaca Muerta development.

Secretaría de Energía — Argentina's energy secretariat, responsible for energy policy, production monitoring, and regulatory oversight. SAyDS (Secretaría de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sustentable) — the federal environment secretariat, responsible for môi trường policy and biến đổi khí hậu. ENARGAS (Ente Nacional Regulador del Gas) — the national gas regulatory authority, overseeing gas transport, distribution, and tariff regulation.

Congreso de la Nación Argentina — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (257 members) and the Senado (72 members). Citizens can contact representatives through diputados.gov.ar and senado.gob.ar. The Comisión de Energía y Combustibles handles energy legislation. Argentina's NDC — Argentina submitted an updated NDC in 2021 targeting phát thải of no more than 349 MtCO₂e by 2030. Climate Action Tracker rates this as 'highly insufficient.'

AT.s: Wealthy EU nền kinh tế ($534.8B GDP) with strong manufacturing (15.1% of GDP). Low direct fossil fuel exposure but significant UPF and financial sector PST. Major transit hub for Russian gas (Baumgarten hub). High healthcare expenditure (11.83% GDP) suggests diet-related disease burden.

sectors.w: Austria's healthcare system (ASVG social insurance) tracks diet-related disease costs with high granularity. Khi per-product-category healthcare costs are published alongside retail revenue, W trở nên có thể tính được from bilateral UPF transactions.
bp: Thực hiện front-of-pack health-impact labelling through EU Farm to Fork Strategy. Khi consumers see welfare costs at point of sale, the lợi ích chồng chéo between UPF manufacturers and retailers in high-margin processed products suy yếu.

Mandate publication of diet-attributable healthcare costs per product category using ASVG social insurance data. Austria's comprehensive health insurance system generates the data needed to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix in ways that affect metabolic response. Cải cáchulation mandates can reduce but not eliminate sự phụ thuộc hệ thống.
a: Report misleading health claims on food products to the Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (Federal Competition Authority)
Document undisclosed processing methods — report to AGES (Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety)
Use the HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (Whistleblower Protection Act, 2023) for protected reporting
Cải cáchulate product lines to reduce ultra-processing — invest in minimally processed alternatives
Voluntarily adopt front-of-pack nutritional labelling ahead of EU mandate
Commission health-impact assessment of product portfolio using national health insurance data
BMSGPK: Thực hiện mandatory front-of-pack health labelling for UPF products
AGES: Publish annual diet-attributable disease burden data linked to specific product categories
Hauptverband der Sozialversicherungsträger: Release anonymized diet-related healthcare cost data
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn member of the Nationalrat or Bundesrat để yêu cầu mandatory health-impact labelling on ultra-processed foods
Support EU-wide adoption of front-of-pack nutritional labelling
Advocate for inclusion of diet-attributable health costs in national accounting
Thúc đẩy WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions through EU legislative process
i: Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (BWB / Federal Competition Authority) — Austria's competition authority, investigating unfair commercial practices including misleading advertising. AGES (Agentur für Gesundheit und Ernährungssicherheit / Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety) — the federal agency responsible for food safety, nutrition surveillance, and public health. AGES conducts food inspections and risk assessments. HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (HSchG, 2023) — Austria's transposition of the EU Whistleblower Directive, providing protection for employees reporting violations of EU or national law.

EU Farm to Fork Strategy — includes proposals for mandatory front-of-pack nutrition labelling across the EU. Austrian food companies that move early gain competitive advantage in the EU single market. EFSA (European Food Safety Authority) — provides scientific opinions that shape EU food regulation applicable in Austria.

BMSGPK (Bundesministerium für Soziales, Gesundheit, Pflege und Konsumentenschutz / Federal Ministry of Social Affairs, Health, Care and Consumer Protection) — oversees food safety policy, nutrition guidelines, and consumer protection. Hauptverband der österreichischen Sozialversicherungsträger (now Dachverband) — the umbrella organization of Austrian social insurance institutions, managing healthcare data for the entire insured population.

Österreichisches Parlament — Austria's bicameral parliament consisting of the Nationalrat (183 members) and the Bundesrat (61 members). Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.gv.at. The Gesundheitsausschuss (Health Committee) handles health and food policy legislation.

AZ.s: Oil-dependent nền kinh tế with 29.57% combined oil and gas rents ($22B). SOCAR (State Oil Company) dominates. Hosted COP29 (2024). Authoritarian governance with limited xã hội dân sự space. Caspian Sea oil and gas fields (ACG, Shah Deniz) are mature.

sectors.w: Azerbaijan's oil production is declining from mature Caspian fields (ACG peaked in 2010). As production costs rise and reserves deplete, the lợi ích chồng chéo between SOCAR and international partners (BP) suy yếu — diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate post-oil diversification using SOFAZ (State Oil Fund) reserves. Azerbaijan's $50B+ sovereign wealth fund can finance năng lượng tái tạo and non-oil economic development. Khi SOFAZ investment returns from renewables exceed declining oil returns, the lợi ích chồng chéo breaks.
Thực hiện theo từng mỏ phát thải disclosure for all Caspian operations. BP already publishes phát thải data for ACG — extending to all SOCAR operations makes W computable across the sector.
Không thể phá vỡ by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report môi trường violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources
Document phát thải underreporting through internal SOCAR channels
Report môi trường concerns to international partners (BP) through their compliance systems
SOCAR: Phát triển binding năng lượng tái tạo transition plan with interim phát thải targets
BP Azerbaijan: Extend ACG phát thải minh bạch standards to all partner operations
SOFAZ: Áp dụng ESG investment criteria excluding new fossil fuel development
Ministry of Ecology: Tăng cường môi trường standards for offshore Caspian operations
EITI Azerbaijan: Expand reporting to include môi trường impact data per production block
Tariff Council: Cải cách energy pricing to remove implicit fossil fuel consumption subsidies
Leverage Azerbaijan's COP29 presidency legacy to strengthen national climate commitments
Vận động qua the Tehran Convention for Caspian Sea môi trường protection standards
Support EITI enhanced reporting with môi trường metrics
Thúc đẩy European Energy Charter Treaty enforcement on môi trường standards in transit countries
i: Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources — Azerbaijan's cơ quan môi trường, responsible for môi trường monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. The Ministry oversees môi trường impact assessments for energy projects. SOCAR (State Oil Company of the Azerbaijan Republic) — the national oil company controlling all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Azerbaijan has no comprehensive người tố giác protection law. Civil society space is severely constrained; independent media and NGOs face significant operational barriers.

SOCAR — Azerbaijan's state oil company, operating the country's refining, petrochemical, and upstream assets. SOCAR also has international operations in Turkey, Georgia, and Europe. BP — the largest international operator in Azerbaijan, managing the Azeri-Chirag-Deepwater Gunashli (ACG) oilfield and Shah Deniz gas field through Production Sharing Agreements. SOFAZ (State Oil Fund of the Republic of Azerbaijan) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Azerbaijan's petroleum revenues, with assets exceeding $50B.

Azerbaijan EITI — Azerbaijan was an early EITI member (2003) and publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues. The EITI process provides one of the few independent minh bạch mechanisms in the sector. Tariff (Price) Council — sets regulated energy prices in Azerbaijan. Tehran Convention — the Framework Convention for the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Caspian Sea, which governs môi trường standards for offshore operations.

Milli Majlis (National Assembly of Azerbaijan) — unicameral parliament with 125 members. Note: Azerbaijan is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House; elections are not considered free and fair by international observers. Civic engagement channels are limited. International pressure operates primarily through multilateral forums including the Council of Europe (Azerbaijan is a member), OSCE, and the EITI Board. The EU-Azerbaijan Partnership and Cooperation Agreement provides a framework for dialogue on energy and môi trường issues.

BA.s: Post-conflict nền kinh tế ($29.6B GDP) with complex governance (two entities: Federation and Republika Srpska). Coal-dependent energy sector and manufacturing (12.7% GDP). ứng cử viên EU country. Significant coal khai thác mỏ and thermal power generation.

sectors.w: Bosnia's coal sector is heavily subsidized through below-market electricity pricing and state employment guarantees. EU accession requires alignment with the EU ETS — when carbon pricing applies, the lợi ích chồng chéo between coal producers and the state employment system breaks.
bp: EU accession alignment with EU ETS carbon pricing. Khi Bosnian coal power must pay the carbon price (~€90/ton), coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive with imported renewables. The lợi ích chồng chéo between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.

Mandatory phát thải monitoring and disclosure per power plant. Energy Community Treaty membership requires EU-aligned môi trường reporting — implementing this makes W computable from electricity generation data.
Không thể phá vỡ. Coal combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report môi trường violations at coal mines and power plants to entity-level môi trường inspectorates
Document health impacts in communities near coal power plants — report to public health institutes
Report corruption in coal procurement to the Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption
State electricity companies (EPBiH, ERS): Phát triển coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community Treaty obligations
Đầu tư vào năng lượng tái tạo capacity to replace aging coal plants
Thực hiện phát thải monitoring per facility ahead of EU ETS alignment
DERK (State Electricity Regulatory Commission): Cải cách tariffs to reflect true môi trường costs of coal generation
Entity môi trường agencies: Thực thi air quality standards at coal power plants
Energy Community Secretariat: Use dispute resolution to enforce môi trường compliance
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn member of the Parlamentarna skupština để yêu cầu coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community obligations
Support EU accession negotiations contingent on energy transition commitments
Advocate for just transition funding for coal-dependent communities through IPA III (EU Instrument for Pre-Accession)
Thúc đẩy Energy Community Treaty enforcement on air quality standards
i: Environmental inspection is split between the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Republika Srpska, each maintaining separate môi trường agencies. Federation: Federalni inspektorat — môi trường inspectorate. Republika Srpska: Republička uprava za inspekcijske poslove — inspection administration. Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption (APIK) — state-level anti-corruption body. Bosnia lacks comprehensive người tố giác protection legislation, though draft laws have been discussed as part of EU accession reforms.

EPBiH (Elektroprivreda Bosne i Hercegovine) — the Federation's state electricity company, operating coal-fired power plants and hydropower. ERS (Elektroprivreda Republike Srpske) — Republika Srpska's state electricity company. Both companies operate aging coal infrastructure (many plants dating to Yugoslav era) and are major employers in khai thác mỏ communities.

DERK (Državna regulatorna komisija za električnu energiju / State Electricity Regulatory Commission) — the state-level electricity regulator. FERK and RERS are entity-level regulators. Energy Community Treaty — Bosnia is a contracting party, committed to implementing EU energy and môi trường acquis including Large Combustion Plants Directive and Industrial Emissions Directive.

Parlamentarna skupština Bosne i Hercegovine (Parliamentary Assembly) — bicameral legislature consisting of the Predstavnički dom (House of Representatives, 42 members) and the Dom naroda (House of Peoples, 15 members). Bosnia's complex governance structure (Dayton Agreement) divides power between state, entity, and cantonal levels. Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.ba.

BD.s: Large nền kinh tế ($450.1B GDP) driven by garment manufacturing (21.9% mfg share of GDP). High agricultural employment (11.2% GDP). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying delta at severe flood and cyclone risk. One of the world's most densely populated countries.

sectors.w: Bangladesh's rice monoculture depletes groundwater (arsenic contamination) and reduces soil biodiversity. Satellite-based crop monitoring and water quality data from BWDB can make W computable from agricultural output and water extraction volumes.
bp: Cải cách agricultural subsidies from input-based (fertilizer, diesel) to outcome-based (soil health, water efficiency). Khi subsidies reward diversification rather than rice intensification, lợi ích chồng chéo trong monoculture between farmers and input suppliers suy yếu.

Mandatory groundwater extraction monitoring and arsenic contamination mapping published at upazila level. BWDB already collects water data — extending to public disclosure linked to agricultural output makes W computable.

Có thể phá vỡ một phần. Intensive rice cultivation in deltaic conditions inherently alters hydrology and nutrient cycling. However, System of Rice Intensification (SRI) and crop diversification can reduce sự phụ thuộc hệ thống.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension)
Document groundwater over-extraction or arsenic contamination — report to DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering)
Report food safety violations in agricultural supply chains to BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority)
Agricultural input companies: Phát triển integrated pest management products as alternatives to chemical-intensive packages
Đầu tư vào climate-resilient crop varieties through partnerships with BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute)
Áp dụng water-use efficiency targets across supply chains
Ministry of Agriculture: Cải cách subsidy structure from input-based to outcome-based
BWDB: Thực hiện mandatory groundwater monitoring networks in intensive agricultural zones
DoE (Department of Environment): Thực thi regulations on agricultural chemical runoff into waterways
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn member of the Jatiya Sangsad để yêu cầu reform of agricultural subsidies toward climate resilience and crop diversification
Support Bangladesh's Climate Vulnerable Forum leadership in advocating for agricultural adaptation finance
Advocate for inclusion of groundwater depletion costs in national GDP accounting
Thúc đẩy international climate finance to support agricultural diversification away from rice monoculture
i: DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension) — the primary government agency for agricultural technical support and extension services, operating at union, upazila, and district levels. DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering) — responsible for water supply and sanitation, including monitoring arsenic contamination in groundwater (affecting ~35 million people). BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority) — established in 2015 to regulate food safety across the supply chain. Bangladesh lacks comprehensive người tố giác protection legislation.

BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute) — the national research institute developing improved rice varieties, including flood-tolerant (BRRI dhan52), salt-tolerant, and drought-resistant varieties. BARI (Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute) — conducts research on non-rice crops including vegetables, pulses, and oilseeds. BADC (Bangladesh Agricultural Phát triểnment Corporation) — the government agency responsible for seed production and distribution.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy, subsidies, and development programs. BWDB (Bangladesh Water Phát triểnment Board) — responsible for flood control, drainage, and irrigation infrastructure. The BWDB manages the country's water resource data system. DoE (Department of Environment) — Bangladesh's môi trường regulatory agency, responsible for môi trường clearance of projects and monitoring pollution.

Jatiya Sangsad (National Parliament of Bangladesh) — unicameral legislature with 350 members (300 directly elected, 50 reserved for women). Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.gov.bd. The Standing Committee on Ministry of Agriculture handles agricultural policy. Bangladesh Climate Change Strategy and Action Plan (BCCSAP) — the national framework for climate adaptation and mitigation, including agricultural resilience.

BE.s: EU and NATO headquarters nền kinh tế ($671.4B GDP). Services-dominant (71% GDP). Major port (Antwerp — Europe's second-largest) handles significant fossil fuel and petrochemical throughput. High healthcare expenditure (10.8% GDP). Federal state with three regions.

sectors.w: Belgium's comprehensive INAMI/RIZIV health insurance system tracks healthcare costs with high granularity. Publishing diet-attributable disease costs per product category makes W computable from retail sales data.
bp: Thực hiện front-of-pack health labelling (Belgium adopted Nutri-Score voluntarily in 2019). Extending to mandatory application and adding health-cost information suy yếu lợi ích chồng chéo trong high-margin UPF.
INAMI/RIZIV publication of anonymized diet-attributable healthcare cost data per product category. Belgium's universal health insurance generates comprehensive data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix affecting metabolic response. Cải cáchulation mandates reduce but do not eliminate sự phụ thuộc hệ thống.
a: Report misleading health claims to SPF Économie (Federal Public Service Economy)
Document food safety violations — report to AFSCA/FAVV (Federal Agency for the Safety of the Food Chain)
Use the Belgian người tố giác protection law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937) for protected reporting
Cải cáchulate product lines to reduce ultra-processing
Extend voluntary Nutri-Score adoption to all product lines
Commission health-impact assessment of product portfolio
SPF Santé publique: Make Nutri-Score mandatory for all packaged food products
AFSCA/FAVV: Tăng cường enforcement of marketing claims on UPF products
INAMI/RIZIV: Publish annual reports linking food consumption patterns to healthcare expenditure
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn member of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers để yêu cầu mandatory health-impact labelling
Support EU-wide mandatory Nutri-Score through the Farm to Fork Strategy
Advocate for WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions
Thúc đẩy inclusion of diet-related health costs in national accounting frameworks
i: AFSCA/FAVV (Agence fédérale pour la sécurité de la chaîne alimentaire / Federaal Agentschap voor de Veiligheid van de Voedselketen) — Belgium's federal food safety agency, responsible for food chain safety from farm to fork. Conducts inspections, sampling, and enforcement. SPF Économie (Service public fédéral Économie) — the federal economic affairs service handling consumer protection and fair commercial practices.

Fevia (Federation of the Belgian Food Industry) — the trade association representing over 700 food companies in Belgium. Fevia's sustainability commitments influence industry practices. Belgium hosts major food companies including Puratos, Vandemoortele, and Lotus Bakeries.

SPF Santé publique (Federal Public Service Health, Food Chain Safety and Environment) — the federal ministry overseeing public health policy, food safety, and environment. INAMI/RIZIV (Institut national d'assurance maladie-invalidité / Rijksinstituut voor ziekte- en invaliditeitsverzekering) — Belgium's national health insurance institute, managing the compulsory health insurance system and healthcare cost data.

Parlement fédéral / Federaal Parlement — Belgium's bicameral parliament consisting of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers (150 members) and the Sénat / Senaat (60 members). Citizens can contact representatives through lachambre.be / dekamer.be. Belgium's federal structure means health policy is shared between federal and regional (community) governments.

BF.s: Low-income Sahelian nền kinh tế ($23.1B GDP) with dominant gold khai thác mỏ sector (min=15.45% GDP). Agriculture employs 80%+ of population (20.9% GDP). Military junta since 2022 coup. Severe security challenges (jihadist insurgency).

sectors.w: Burkina Faso's gold khai thác mỏ generates cyanide contamination, mercury pollution (artisanal khai thác mỏ), and community displacement. Khi môi trường and health costs per mine are published alongside production revenue, W trở nên có thể tính được.
bp: Mandate môi trường bonds and community benefit agreements for all industrial khai thác mỏ licenses. Khi khai thác mỏ companies must pre-fund môi trường remediation and community compensation, lợi ích chồng chéo trong extraction without accountability suy yếu.

Publish theo từng mỏ môi trường impact data (water quality, air quality, land disturbance) alongside production revenue through EITI reporting. Burkina Faso's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to môi trường data makes W visible.

Có thể phá vỡ một phần. Open-pit gold khai thác mỏ inherently disturbs landscapes and generates toxic waste. However, improved tailings management and mercury-free artisanal methods can reduce sự phụ thuộc hệ thống.
a: Report môi trường violations at mine sites to BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales)
Document community health impacts near khai thác mỏ operations through local health clinics
Report illegal artisanal khai thác mỏ activities and mercury use to provincial authorities
Mining companies (Endeavour Mining, IAMGOLD, Nordgold): Thực hiện Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management
Áp dụng EITI enhanced reporting including môi trường impact metrics
Đầu tư vào mercury-free processing technologies for artisanal khai thác mỏ support programs
Ministry of Mines: Tăng cường môi trường conditions in khai thác mỏ permits and enforce compliance
BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie): Improve artisanal khai thác mỏ regulation to reduce mercury use
EITI-BF: Expand reporting to include môi trường and community impact data
Vận động qua ECOWAS for regional khai thác mỏ môi trường standards
Support African Mining Vision implementation for sustainable resource governance
Thúc đẩy international khai thác mỏ companies to adopt binding môi trường standards through OECD Due Diligence Guidance
Support Kimberley Process-style certification for gold supply chains to reduce conflict mineral financing
i: BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales) — Burkina Faso's national môi trường assessment authority, responsible for môi trường impact assessments of khai thác mỏ projects. Ministère de l'Environnement, de l'Énergie, de l'Eau et de l'Assainissement — oversees môi trường policy and regulation. Note: Under military rule since 2022, institutional capacity is constrained and xã hội dân sự space is restricted.

Major khai thác mỏ companies operating in Burkina Faso include Endeavour Mining (Houndé, Wahgnion mines), IAMGOLD (Essakane mine), and Nordgold (Bissa-Bouly). These international companies are subject to home-country ESG reporting requirements and international khai thác mỏ standards (ICMM, IFC Performance Standards).

Ministère des Mines et des Carrières — the ministry responsible for khai thác mỏ policy, licensing, and regulation. BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie du Burkina) — the geological survey and khai thác mỏ technical agency. EITI Burkina Faso — Burkina Faso has been an EITI member since 2009, publishing reports on khai thác mỏ revenues. The 2015 Mining Code establishes môi trường obligations for khai thác mỏ companies.

Note: Burkina Faso is under military rule (Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré since 2022). The Assemblée Législative de Transition (Transitional Legislative Assembly) replaced the elected National Assembly. Democratic institutions are suspended. International engagement channels: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States — though Burkina Faso announced withdrawal in 2024), African Union, and international development partners. The African Mining Vision (2009) provides a continental framework for sustainable khai thác mỏ governance.

BG.s: thành viên EU nền kinh tế ($113.3B GDP) with legacy coal dependence (Maritsa East complex is one of Europe's largest lignite basins). Services-dominant (63.8% GDP). EU accession has driven institutional modernization but coal transition remains politically sensitive.

sectors.w: Bulgaria's coal sector is sustained by implicit subsidies (below-market electricity pricing, state employment). EU ETS carbon pricing makes lignite uncompetitive — when full carbon costs are internalized, the lợi ích chồng chéo between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.
bp: Full alignment with EU ETS free allowance phase-out. Khi Bulgarian lignite plants pay the EU carbon price without free allowances, coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive. The lợi ích chồng chéo breaks when renewable alternatives are cheaper.

Mandatory theo từng nhà máy phát thải and health-impact disclosure. Bulgaria's lignite plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) are among Europe's worst air polluters. Publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable from electricity generation data.
Không thể phá vỡ. Lignite combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, NOx, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report phát thải violations at coal plants to RIOSV (Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water)
Document health impacts in communities near Maritsa East complex — report to RZI (Regional Health Inspectorates)
Use Bulgaria's người tố giác protection law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937) for protected reporting
Maritsa East complex operators: Phát triển coal phase-out plans with worker transition programs
Đầu tư vào năng lượng tái tạo capacity (Bulgaria has strong solar potential — 1,500+ kWh/m²/year)
Áp dụng per-unit phát thải reporting aligned with EU taxonomy
KEVR (Energy and Water Regulatory Commission): Cải cách electricity tariffs to reflect carbon costs
Ministry of Energy: Phát triển binding coal phase-out timeline aligned with EU Green Deal
Ministry of Environment: Thực thi Industrial Emissions Directive compliance at all coal plants
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn member of the Narodno Sabranie (National Assembly) để yêu cầu a binding coal phase-out timeline with just transition funding
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Stara Zagora and other coal-dependent regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS free allowance phase-out
Thúc đẩy Territorial Just Transition Plans that include concrete retraining and economic diversification measures
i: RIOSV (Регионални инспекции по околната среда и водите / Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water) — 16 regional môi trường agencies conducting inspections and enforcement. EEA (Изпълнителна агенция по околна среда / Executive Environment Agency) — the national môi trường monitoring agency collecting phát thải and air quality data. Bulgaria transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2023.

Maritsa East complex — comprises three lignite-fired power plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) and the associated Maritsa-Iztok mines. AES Bulgaria operates Maritsa East I; ContourGlobal operates Maritsa East III. The complex is the largest industrial employer in the Stara Zagora region. Mini Maritsa-Iztok — the nhà nước lignite khai thác mỏ company supplying the Maritsa East plants.

KEVR (Комисия за енергийно и водно регулиране / Energy and Water Regulatory Commission) — Bulgaria's independent cơ quan quản lý năng lượng, setting electricity tariffs and licensing. Ministry of Energy — oversees energy policy including the National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP). Bulgaria's NECP has been criticized for insufficient coal phase-out ambition.

Narodno Sabranie (Народно събрание / National Assembly of Bulgaria) — unicameral parliament with 240 members. Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.bg. The Комисия по енергетика (Energy Committee) handles energy legislation. EU Just Transition Fund — Bulgaria is eligible for significant JTF funding to support coal transition in affected regions. Bulgaria's Territorial Just Transition Plans cover Stara Zagora, Kyustendil, and Pernik regions.

BH.s: Small island petrostate ($47.1B GDP) with 16.64% combined oil and gas rents. Bahrain's Abu Safa offshore field (shared with Saudi Arabia) provides the majority of oil revenue. Diversifying into financial services (Bahrain Financial Harbour) and aluminum (Alba smelter).

sectors.w: Bahrain's oil reserves are the smallest among GCC states (declining production from mature fields). The economic case for diversification is strongest here — when post-oil planning makes renewable investment more attractive than marginal barrel extraction, the lợi ích chồng chéo breaks.
bp: Accelerate Economic Vision 2030 diversification away from oil dependence. Bahrain's small size and advanced financial sector make it the most transition-ready GCC nền kinh tế. Khi financial services and technology revenue exceeds oil revenue, lợi ích chồng chéo trong extraction breaks.
Publish theo từng mỏ môi trường and phát thải data for all Bahrain Petroleum Company (BAPCO) operations. Making lifecycle phát thải visible per barrel makes W computable from production data.
Không thể phá vỡ by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report môi trường violations at BAPCO facilities to the Supreme Council for Environment
Document phát thải or safety concerns through internal BAPCO compliance channels
Report concerns to Bahrain's National Audit Office regarding mismanagement of petroleum revenues
BAPCO: Accelerate the BAPCO Modernization Program to include năng lượng tái tạo components
Mumtalakat (sovereign wealth fund): Increase năng lượng tái tạo allocation in investment portfolio
Thực hiện TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all operations
NOGA: Require phát thải disclosure for all petroleum operations
SCE: Tăng cường môi trường standards for refinery operations
CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain): Integrate climate risk disclosure into financial sector regulation
Vận động qua GCC Secretariat for regional carbon pricing mechanisms
Support IRENA recommendations on GCC energy transition pathways
Thúc đẩy enhanced climate disclosure standards through the Bahrain Bourse ESG framework
Leverage Bahrain's UNFCCC commitments to strengthen national phát thải reduction targets
i: Supreme Council for Environment (SCE) — Bahrain's cơ quan môi trường, responsible for môi trường policy, permitting, and enforcement. SCE oversees môi trường impact assessments for industrial projects. BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — the nhà nước oil company operating Bahrain's refinery and upstream assets. National Audit Office — conducts financial audits of government entities including BAPCO. Note: Bahrain has limited người tố giác protection; xã hội dân sự space is constrained.

BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — operates the Sitra refinery (267,000 bpd after modernization) and manages upstream production. Mumtalakat — Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund, managing state assets including BAPCO and Alba (Aluminium Bahrain). Nogaholding — the investment arm of Bahrain's National Oil and Gas Authority, overseeing the country's energy sector investments.

NOGA (National Oil and Gas Authority) — the government body responsible for oil and gas policy, licensing, and upstream regulation. CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain) — the financial sector regulator, which has begun integrating ESG considerations into banking supervision. Bahrain Economic Phát triểnment Board — coordinates the Economic Vision 2030 diversification strategy.

Council of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab) — the elected lower house of Bahrain's parliament (40 members) and the Shura Council (Majlis al-Shura, 40 appointed members). Note: Bahrain is a quân chủ lập hiến with significant royal authority. Political opposition is restricted. The Council of Representatives has limited legislative power. International engagement on climate issues operates through GCC coordination and UNFCCC processes.

BJ.s: Small West African nền kinh tế ($21.5B GDP) with dominant agriculture sector (24.2% GDP, primarily cotton). Services growing (48.9% GDP). Cotton is the main export crop — Benin is Africa's largest cotton producer. Climate vulnerability through Sahel desertification and coastal erosion.

sectors.w: Benin's cotton monoculture degrades soil fertility and increases pesticide exposure. Khi soil health data and pesticide-related healthcare costs are published alongside cotton production revenue, W trở nên có thể tính được from agricultural output data.
bp: Cải cách cotton subsidies to reward soil health and crop diversification. Khi input subsidies (seeds, fertilizer, pesticides) are redirected from cotton intensification to mixed farming systems, the lợi ích chồng chéo between cotton traders and input suppliers suy yếu.

Mandatory publication of soil health metrics (organic matter, nutrient balance) and pesticide-related health costs per cotton-producing commune. Making môi trường and health costs visible makes W computable.

Có thể phá vỡ một phần. Cotton monoculture inherently depletes soil nutrients and requires pesticide application. However, integrated pest management and organic cotton production can reduce sự phụ thuộc hệ thống — Benin has a growing organic cotton sector.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles)
Document health impacts of pesticide exposure in cotton-growing communities — report to local health centers
Report corruption in cotton sector management to the Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption (FONAC)
AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton): Promote integrated pest management to reduce chemical dependency
Đầu tư vào organic cotton certification and premium markets
Áp dụng soil health monitoring across cotton supply chains
MAEP (Ministry of Agriculture): Cải cách cotton input subsidy programs to incentivize sustainable practices
ABE: Tăng cường môi trường impact monitoring in cotton-growing zones
INSAE (National Statistics Institute): Include soil health and agricultural môi trường indicators in national surveys
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn Député in the Assemblée Nationale để yêu cầu reform of cotton input subsidies toward sustainable agriculture
Vận động qua ECOWAS for regional agricultural sustainability standards
Thúc đẩy Cotton-4 (Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, Mali) coordination on sustainable cotton production
Support WTO negotiations on cotton subsidy reform to level the playing field for African producers
i: DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles) — the government directorate responsible for agricultural product quality and standards. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — Benin's môi trường agency, responsible for môi trường impact assessment and monitoring. FONAC (Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption) — a national anti-corruption coalition of xã hội dân sự organizations.

AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton) — the inter-professional cotton association coordinating relations between producers, ginners, and input suppliers. SODECO (Société de Développement du Coton) — the main cotton ginning company. The cotton sector was partially liberalized but remains state-coordinated through the AIC framework.

MAEP (Ministère de l'Agriculture, de l'Élevage et de la Pêche) — Benin's agriculture ministry, responsible for agricultural policy and cotton sector coordination. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — conducts môi trường monitoring and impact assessments. INSAE (Institut National de la Statistique et de l'Analyse Économique) — Benin's national statistics institute.

Assemblée Nationale du Bénin — unicameral parliament with 109 members. Citizens can contact their deputies through the official Assembly website. Note: Benin's democratic space has narrowed under recent constitutional reforms, though elections continue. Cotton-4 group — Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, and Mali coordinate on cotton trade policy at the WTO, advocating against subsidies in developed countries that depress global cotton prices.

BN.s: Small wealthy petrostate ($15.3B GDP) with 24.23% combined oil and gas rents. Brunei Shell Petroleum (BSP, 50/50 JV with Shell) dominates the nền kinh tế. Absolute monarchy. Wawasan Brunei 2035 diversification strategy underway but oil/gas remain 60%+ of government revenue.

sectors.w: Brunei's oil production is in long-term decline (from 220,000 bpd peak to ~100,000 bpd). As reserves deplete, the lợi ích chồng chéo between BSP and the government in continued extraction suy yếu — Wawasan 2035 diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate Wawasan 2035 economic diversification using Brunei Investment Agency (BIA) sovereign wealth. Khi non-oil sectors generate more government revenue than declining oil production, lợi ích chồng chéo trong extraction breaks.
Thực hiện theo từng mỏ phát thải disclosure for all BSP and Total operations. Shell's global ESG reporting standards can be extended to Brunei operations to make W computable.
Không thể phá vỡ by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report môi trường violations at BSP facilities through Shell's global ethics and compliance channels
Document phát thải data gaps — report to the Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation
Use BSP's internal reporting mechanisms for safety and môi trường concerns
BSP/Shell: Extend Shell's global net-zero commitments to Brunei operations
BIA (Brunei Investment Agency): Diversify sovereign wealth into năng lượng tái tạo and technology
Thực hiện TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all Brunei operations
PetroleumBRUNEI: Require phát thải monitoring and disclosure for all production licenses
Department of Environment: Tăng cường môi trường impact assessment requirements
AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam): Integrate climate risk into financial supervision
Vận động qua ASEAN for regional năng lượng tái tạo integration and phát thải reduction targets
Support UNFCCC mechanisms for small petrostate transition planning
Thúc đẩy APEC energy working group initiatives on fossil fuel subsidy reform
Leverage Brunei's ASEAN chairmanship rotations to advance regional climate commitments
i: BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — a 50/50 joint venture between the government of Brunei and Shell, the country's largest oil and gas producer. BSP operates onshore and offshore fields. Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation — under the Ministry of Phát triểnment, responsible for môi trường policy and regulation. Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy with no elected legislature. There is no người tố giác protection legislation. Reporting channels are primarily internal corporate mechanisms.

BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — managed jointly by the Brunei government and Shell. BIA (Brunei Investment Agency) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Brunei's petroleum revenues (estimated $30-40B). Total E&P Borneo — operates deep water gas fields offshore Brunei. PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority overseeing the sector.

PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority responsible for policy, regulation, and management of Brunei's oil and gas resources. AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam) — Brunei's central bank and financial regulator. Department of Energy — under the Prime Minister's Office, coordinates energy policy and the Wawasan 2035 energy diversification strategy.

Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy (Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah). There is no elected legislature — the Legislative Council (Majlis Mesyuarat Negara) is appointed and has advisory functions only. Civic engagement operates through petition to the Sultan and through ASEAN and international multilateral channels. ASEAN — Brunei is a founding member. ASEAN's Framework for Circular Economy and declaration on biến đổi khí hậu provide regional engagement mechanisms.

BO.s: Resource-dependent nền kinh tế ($54.9B GDP) with significant natural gas reserves (gas=1.85% rents) and khai thác mỏ (min=5.91%, lithium in Salar de Uyuni — world's largest deposit). Nông nghiệp ở mức 8.8% GDP with soy expansion driving phá rừng. Gas production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Bolivia holds the world's largest lithium reserves in the Salar de Uyuni. Lithium extraction uses massive water volumes in arid regions. Khi water consumption and brine chemistry data are published alongside production revenue, W trở nên có thể tính được.
bp: Require community benefit agreements and water rights guarantees for all lithium extraction. Khi indigenous communities (who hold constitutional land rights under Bolivia's 2009 Constitution) can negotiate fair terms, lợi ích chồng chéo trong extraction without consent suy yếu.

Mandatory publication of water extraction volumes, brine chemistry changes, and downstream ecosystem data per lithium operation. Making môi trường costs visible makes W computable from production data.

Có thể phá vỡ một phần. Lithium brine extraction inherently alters aquifer chemistry and reduces water availability in arid ecosystems. However, direct lithium extraction (DLE) technology can significantly reduce water consumption compared to evaporation ponds.
a: Report môi trường violations at khai thác mỏ operations to the Autoridad de Fiscalización y Control Social de Bosques y Tierra (ABT)
Document water contamination or indigenous rights violations — report to the Defensoría del Pueblo
Report safety violations at khai thác mỏ sites to the Ministry of Labor
YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos): Áp dụng DLE technology to minimize water consumption
Thực hiện community benefit sharing agreements with affected indigenous communities
Áp dụng international sustainability standards (IRMA — Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance)
Ministry of Mining: Tăng cường môi trường requirements for lithium extraction permits
MMAyA (Ministry of Environment and Water): Thực thi water extraction limits in Salar de Uyuni basin
AJAM: Require môi trường bonds for all khai thác mỏ concessions
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn Diputado or Senador in the Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional để yêu cầu mandatory prior consultation with indigenous communities for all lithium projects
Support ILO Convention 169 enforcement on indigenous consultation rights in extractive industries
Advocate for international standards on sustainable lithium extraction through the Lithium Triangle coordination (Bolivia, Argentina, Chile)
Thúc đẩy inclusion of water depletion costs in khai thác mỏ revenue accounting
i: AJAM (Autoridad Jurisdiccional Administrativa Minera) — the khai thác mỏ administrative authority responsible for granting and overseeing khai thác mỏ concessions. Defensoría del Pueblo — Bolivia's human rights ombudsman, responsible for investigating complaints about government actions affecting fundamental rights, including indigenous and môi trường rights. Bolivia's 2009 Constitution (Article 30) — guarantees indigenous peoples' right to prior consultation on resource extraction in their territories.

YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos) — Bolivia's state lithium company, managing lithium industrialization in the Salar de Uyuni. COMIBOL (Corporación Minera de Bolivia) — the state khai thác mỏ corporation overseeing Bolivia's khai thác mỏ sector. YPFB (Yacimientos Petrolíferos Fiscales Bolivianos) — the state hydrocarbons company.

Ministerio de Minería y Metalurgia — Bolivia's khai thác mỏ ministry, responsible for khai thác mỏ policy and regulation. MMAyA (Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Agua) — the environment and water ministry, overseeing môi trường policy and water resource management. SENARECOM (Servicio Nacional de Registro y Control de la Comercialización de Minerales y Metales) — the national service for mineral commerce registration and control.

Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional (Plurinational Legislative Assembly) — Bolivia's bicameral parliament consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (130 members) and the Cámara de Senadores (36 members). Bolivia's 2009 Constitution establishes a 'plurinational' state recognizing indigenous rights and communitarian dân chủ. ILO Convention 169 — Bolivia has ratified ILO Convention 169 on Indigenous and Tribal Peoples, requiring free, prior, and informed consultation on resource extraction in indigenous territories.

BS.s: Small island nền kinh tế ($15.8B GDP) dominated by tourism and financial services (77.2% services). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying islands face existential risk from sea level rise and intensifying hurricanes (Hurricane Dorian, 2019, caused $3.4B damage — 25% of GDP).

sectors.w: The Bahamas' offshore financial center enables capital flows that may avoid tax obligations elsewhere. Khi beneficial ownership information and tax minh bạch data are published, the system welfare impact of offshore finance becomes computable.
bp: Thực hiện full beneficial ownership minh bạch in line with FATF recommendations. Khi the competitive advantage of Bahamian financial secrecy is eliminated through minh bạch, the lợi ích chồng chéo between offshore service providers and tax-avoiding clients suy yếu.

Publish aggregate data on capital flows, tax rulings, and beneficial ownership through enhanced OECD Common Reporting Standard participation. Making tax revenue impacts visible to source jurisdictions makes W computable.

Có thể phá vỡ một phần. Financial intermediation can relocate — but the Bahamas' geographic proximity to the US and established legal infrastructure create switching costs that give regulation leverage.
a: Report suspicious transactions to the Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) of The Bahamas
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to the Securities Commission of The Bahamas
Report regulatory violations to the Central Bank of The Bahamas
Áp dụng enhanced due diligence procedures for all client relationships
Thực hiện beneficial ownership minh bạch standards ahead of regulatory mandates
Integrate climate risk assessment into financial products and client advisory services
Central Bank: Tăng cường beneficial ownership disclosure requirements for all licensed financial institutions
Securities Commission: Thực thi FATF recommendations on minh bạch and anti-money laundering
Compliance Commission: Increase supervision of designated non-financial businesses and professions
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn Member of Parliament in the House of Assembly để yêu cầu enhanced financial minh bạch legislation
Support OECD Base Erosion and Profit Shifting (BEPS) Inclusive Framework participation
Leverage the Alliance of Small Island States (AOSIS) to link climate finance to financial minh bạch commitments
Advocate for Caribbean regional coordination on beneficial ownership minh bạch
i: FIU (Financial Intelligence Unit of The Bahamas) — the agency responsible for receiving, analyzing, and disseminating suspicious transaction reports. The FIU works with law enforcement to combat money laundering and terrorist financing. Securities Commission of The Bahamas — regulates the investment funds industry and securities market. Central Bank of The Bahamas — the prudential regulator of banks and trust companies.

The Bahamas is home to approximately 200 licensed banks and trust companies. Major international banks maintain operations in Nassau. The Bahamas Dịch vụ Tài chính Board (BFSB) promotes the financial services sector and coordinates industry standards.

Central Bank of The Bahamas — the primary financial regulator and supervisor. The Compliance Commission — supervises non-bank financial service providers for anti-money laundering compliance. FATF (Financial Action Task Force) — The Bahamas has been subject to FATF enhanced monitoring and has implemented reforms to address identified deficiencies in its AML/CFT framework.

Parliament of The Bahamas — bicameral legislature consisting of the House of Assembly (39 members) and the Senate (16 appointed members). Citizens can contact their MP through the official government portal. The Bahamas is a Westminster-style nghị viện dân chủ. AOSIS (Alliance of Small Island States) — The Bahamas is an active member, advocating for climate action and sea level rise mitigation. CARICOM — provides regional coordination on financial regulation.

BW.s: Upper-middle-income nền kinh tế ($19.4B GDP) with diamond khai thác mỏ historically driving growth (Debswana JV with De Beers). Services-dominant (63.5% GDP). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Diversification challenge as lab-grown diamonds threaten natural diamond demand.

sectors.w: Lab-grown diamonds are disrupting the natural diamond market (30%+ of gem market by volume). As synthetic alternatives become indistinguishable and cheaper, the lợi ích chồng chéo between Debswana and consumers in natural diamond premium suy yếu — Botswana must diversify.
bp: Accelerate economic diversification using Pula Fund sovereign wealth. Khi non-diamond revenue streams (tech, tourism, beef) exceed declining diamond revenues, lợi ích chồng chéo trong continued diamond dependence breaks naturally.

Publish full lifecycle môi trường data (water use, land disturbance, energy consumption) per carat for all khai thác mỏ operations. Making môi trường costs visible makes W computable from diamond production data.

Có thể phá vỡ một phần. Open-pit diamond khai thác mỏ inherently disturbs landscapes. However, Botswana's mines are in relatively low-biodiversity Kalahari terrain, and rehabilitation programs can reduce long-term sự phụ thuộc hệ thống.
a: Report môi trường violations at mine sites to DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs)
Document community displacement or inadequate compensation — report to the Ombudsman
Report safety violations to the Department of Mines
Debswana: Phát triển post-diamond economic transition plan with năng lượng tái tạo investment
De Beers: Extend lifecycle môi trường reporting to all Botswana operations
Áp dụng Kimberley Process Certification Scheme enhanced standards including môi trường metrics
Ministry of Minerals and Energy: Require môi trường bonds for all khai thác mỏ licenses
BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service): Ensure mineral royalties reflect true môi trường costs
Okavango Diamond Company: Lead on sustainability certification for Botswana diamonds
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn Member of Parliament in the National Assembly để yêu cầu a comprehensive post-diamond economic diversification strategy
Support African Union Mining Vision implementation for sustainable diamond governance
Advocate for Kimberley Process reform to include môi trường and social standards
Thúc đẩy SADC (Southern African Phát triểnment Community) coordination on khai thác mỏ sustainability
i: DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs) — under the Ministry of Environment and Tourism, responsible for môi trường policy, impact assessment, and monitoring. Department of Mines — under the Ministry of Minerals and Energy, responsible for khai thác mỏ safety regulation, licensing, and compliance monitoring. Office of the Ombudsman — investigates complaints against government agencies and public officials.

Debswana Diamond Company — a 50/50 joint venture between the Government of Botswana and De Beers Group, operating the Jwaneng and Orapa mines. Debswana is Botswana's largest private employer and the largest contributor to government revenue. De Beers Group (Anglo American subsidiary) — the global diamond company partnering with Botswana. The 2023 renegotiation of the Debswana agreement gave Botswana a greater share of rough diamond sales.

Ministry of Minerals and Energy — oversees khai thác mỏ policy and the regulatory framework under the Mines and Minerals Act. BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service) — the tax authority responsible for collecting mineral royalties and taxes. Pula Fund — Botswana's sovereign wealth fund (managed by the Bank of Botswana), designed to save diamond revenues for future generations.

National Assembly of Botswana — unicameral parliament with 65 members (57 elected, 8 specially elected). Citizens can contact their MPs through the nghị viện website. Botswana is one of Africa's most stable multiparty democracies, with peaceful transfers of power since independence in 1966. The Ntlo ya Dikgosi (House of Chiefs) — an advisory body representing traditional leaders, with input on matters affecting tribal territories including khai thác mỏ.

BY.s: State-controlled nền kinh tế ($76B GDP) with significant manufacturing (20.3% GDP). Potash khai thác mỏ (Belaruskali is one of the world's largest producers). Oil refining (Naftan, Mozyr) processes Russian crude. Authoritarian governance under Lukashenko since 1994.

sectors.w: Belarus is the world's third-largest potash producer (Belaruskali). Potash khai thác mỏ generates massive salt waste (solukhards) that contaminate groundwater and rivers. Khi downstream water contamination data is published alongside potash production revenue, W trở nên có thể tính được.
bp: International trừng phạt (EU, US, UK since 2021) have already weakened the lợi ích chồng chéo by restricting Belaruskali's market access. Khi sanctioned export revenues fall below môi trường remediation costs, the economic case for continued expansion breaks.

Satellite-based môi trường monitoring (salt waste accumulation, water contamination plumes) published by international organizations. Making môi trường damage visible from space bypasses domestic data suppression and makes W computable.

Có thể phá vỡ một phần. Potash khai thác mỏ inherently generates salt waste. However, solution khai thác mỏ and backfill techniques can reduce surface waste accumulation and groundwater contamination.
a: Document môi trường violations through secure channels to international môi trường organizations
Report safety concerns through ILO complaint mechanisms
Provide data to international monitoring organizations (Belarusian diaspora networks)
International potash buyers: Demand môi trường certification as condition of purchase (post-trừng phạt)
Apply IFC Performance Standards to any future investment in Belarusian khai thác mỏ
Require independent môi trường audits as part of off-take agreements
International trừng phạt bodies: Maintain and strengthen potash export trừng phạt until môi trường and governance reforms are implemented
UNECE: Continue môi trường performance reviews of Belarus under the Convention on Environmental Impact Assessment (Espoo Convention)
IndustriALL: Monitor labor rights conditions at Belaruskali
Support maintenance of international trừng phạt on Belaruskali until môi trường and governance reforms are implemented
Vận động qua UNECE for transboundary môi trường monitoring of potash khai thác mỏ impacts
Support Belarusian democratic opposition's platform for môi trường and governance reform
Thúc đẩy international môi trường damage assessment of Soligorsk khai thác mỏ district
i: Note: Belarus under Lukashenko is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. Independent media, xã hội dân sự, and trade unions face severe repression. There is no effective người tố giác protection. After the 2020 protests, thousands were imprisoned and major independent organizations were forcibly liquidated. Safe reporting channels include the Belarusian diaspora network, international trade union federations (IndustriALL Global Union), and international môi trường organizations.

Belaruskali — Belarus's nhà nước potash company, one of the world's largest potash producers. Belaruskali operates six mines near Soligorsk. The company has been under EU, US, and UK trừng phạt since 2021 following the forced diversion of Ryanair Flight 4978 and repression of democratic opposition. BPC (Belarusian Potash Company) — the former exclusive export trading arm of Belaruskali (sanctioned). Competitors include Nutrien (Canada), Mosaic (US), and K+S (Germany).

Note: Domestic regulatory institutions (Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Protection) are not independent under chuyên quyền governance. International mechanisms include: UNECE (United Nations Economic Commission for Europe) — Belarus is party to the Espoo Convention and the Convention on the Protection and Use of Transboundary Watercourses. EU trừng phạt framework — Council Regulation (EU) restricting trade with Belaruskali and associated entities.

Note: Belarus has no functioning democratic legislature. The National Assembly (Natsionalnoye Sobranie) is not freely elected. International engagement channels: EU Eastern Partnership framework, OSCE (Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe), United Nations Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review. The Belarusian democratic opposition in exile (led by Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya) maintains a platform including môi trường governance reform.

CD.s: Resource-rich but impoverished nền kinh tế ($71B GDP). Mining dominates (28.81% of GDP) — world's largest cobalt producer (70%+ global supply), significant copper, coltan, and tin. Conflict minerals remain a governance challenge. Agricultural employment is 60%+ but only 9.6% of GDP.

sectors.w: DRC's cobalt is essential for EV batteries — downstream manufacturers (Tesla, Samsung SDI, CATL) face increasing ESG pressure. Khi supply chain traceability makes mine-level môi trường and labor conditions visible to end consumers, W trở nên có thể tính được from cobalt prices.
bp: Mandatory supply chain due diligence (EU Battery Regulation, US Dodd-Frank Section 1502) that requires full traceability to mine site. Khi artisanal miners must meet labor and môi trường standards to access global markets, lợi ích chồng chéo trong unregulated extraction suy yếu.

Blockchain or RMI-style traceability systems linking theo từng mỏ môi trường and labor data to each cobalt lot. The EU Battery Regulation (effective 2027) will require carbon footprint declarations and due diligence — making W computable per unit of cobalt.

Có thể phá vỡ một phần. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes and generates waste. However, formalized artisanal khai thác mỏ with proper safety and môi trường management can reduce sự phụ thuộc hệ thống compared to unregulated extraction.
a: Report môi trường or labor violations at mine sites to international monitoring organizations (IPIS, Global Witness)
Document child labor or unsafe working conditions — report through responsible sourcing audit channels (RMI, LBMA)
Report illegal mineral exports to CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales)
Glencore, CMOC (China Moly): Thực hiện full supply chain traceability to mine site for all DRC cobalt
Áp dụng IRMA (Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance) certification for industrial operations
Đầu tư vào formalization programs for artisanal khai thác mỏ communities
Ministry of Mines: Thực thi 2018 Mining Code môi trường provisions including community development obligations
CEEC: Tăng cường mineral certification to include môi trường and labor compliance data
Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière (CTCPM): Improve artisanal khai thác mỏ registration and oversight
Liên hệ đại biểu của bạn Député national or Sénateur in the Parlement để yêu cầu enforcement of the 2018 Mining Code môi trường provisions
Support EU Battery Regulation implementation requiring full cobalt supply chain traceability
Vận động qua the African Union for continental responsible khai thác mỏ standards
Thúc đẩy OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for all minerals sourced from DRC
i: CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales précieuses et semi-précieuses) — the DRC government agency responsible for evaluating, certifying, and controlling mineral exports. SAEMAPE (Service d'Assistance et d'Encadrement du Small Scale Mining) — the agency responsible for regulating artisanal and small-scale khai thác mỏ. IPIS (International Peace Information Service) — an independent research institute mapping khai thác mỏ sites, armed group presence, and supply chain risks in the DRC. Global Witness — international NGO investigating the links between natural resource exploitation and conflict.
